articles.title: Red cards can be given to players who cover their mouths while confronting opponents
articles.summary: The International Football Association Board approved a rule that would penalize players with a red card if they cover their mouths when confronting another player. The measure will be in place at thi

articles.title: U.S. to issue commemorative passports with Trump's picture for America's 250th birthday
articles.summary: The State Department said that it is preparing a limited release of commemorative U.S. passports celebrating America's 250th birthday that feature a picture of President Donald Trump.

articles.title: Australia moves to tax Meta, Google and TikTok to fund newsrooms
articles.summary: Australia is proposing to tax Meta, Google and TikTok a proportion of their revenue to pay for news reporters. The government intends to introduce the draft legislation to Parliament by July.

articles.title: At State Dinner, King Charles Charms the Court of Trump
articles.summary: King Charles III presented President Trump with a golden bell. “Should you ever need to get hold of us,” the king said, “well, just give us a ring!”

articles.title: Beneath King Charles’s Jokes and Decorum, Some Subtle Rebuttals to Trump
articles.summary: King Charles III gently pushed back against President Trump’s attacks on Britain and NATO, and he spoke of the importance of checks and balances.

articles.title: Full Guest List for Trump’s State Dinner With Charles and Camilla
articles.summary: Six members of the Supreme Court were invited to the dinner, alongside top administration officials, billionaires and Republican lawmakers.

articles.title: See the Menu for the British Royal State Dinner
articles.summary: Spring-herbed ravioli and Dover sole meunière are among the dishes at the dinner honoring King Charles III of Britain and Queen Camilla.

articles.title: DOJ Secures New James Comey Indictment Over Alleged Threat Against Trump
articles.summary: The new case stems from a social media post showing seashells on a North Carolina beach that the Trump administration characterized as a threat against the president.

articles.title: OpenAI Trial Starts With Two Very Different Tales of a Company’s Early Years
articles.summary: In the trial’s first day of testimony, Elon Musk said greed led co-founder Sam Altman to pull the A.I. lab away from its nonprofit roots. OpenAI says that’s nonsense.

articles.title: Yomif Kejelcha broke the 2-hour marathon but got 2nd place. He's still happy
articles.summary: Ethiopia's Yomif Kejelcha ran the London Marathon in under two hours, but he only got second place. He told NPR he hopes to run his next marathon a minute faster.

articles.title: Scientists see Trump's firing of the National Science Board as an attack on research
articles.summary: The move follows an administration push for cuts to the NSF and raises concerns in the scientific community that it could jeopardize a tradition of independent decisions about federal science grants.

articles.title: Linda McMahon punches back at senators questioning Education Department cuts
articles.summary: In her first appearance on Capitol Hill this year, lawmakers questioned Education Secretary Linda McMahon about students' civil rights and cuts to federal education spending.

articles.title: FCC Orders a Review of ABC’s Licenses Amid Feud Between Trump and Kimmel
articles.summary: The agency said the review was related to the network’s diversity and inclusion policies. But it came amid a fight between the president and the network’s late night host, Jimmy Kimmel.

articles.title: With no radical footprint, what drove suspect to try and assassinate Trump?
articles.summary: An attempted shooting at the White House Correspondents Dinner on Saturday has, again, highlighted the climate of political violence in the U.S. But there are still many questions about the motive.

articles.title: FCC orders early license renewal for ABC stations following Kimmel's first lady joke
articles.summary: The Federal Communications Commission has ordered Disney's ABC to seek early broadcast license renewals for the eight TV stations it owns amid backlash over Jimmy Kimmel's joke about Melania Trump.

articles.title: King Charles urges 'reconciliation' in address to U.S. Congress amid Trump tensions
articles.summary: In an address to the U.S. Congress, King Charles told lawmakers that "time and again, our two countries have always found ways to come together," and called for "reconciliation and renewal."

articles.title: What to Know About the Kimmel vs. Trump Feud
articles.summary: The president and first lady took exception to a joke. Jimmy Kimmel defended it.

articles.title: South Korean court extends prison sentence for wife of ousted president
articles.summary: In January, Kim Keon Hee was sentenced to 20 months for accepting gifts from the Unification Church, which sought political favors.

articles.title: The United Arab Emirates is quitting OPEC oil cartel after nearly 60 years
articles.summary: The Arab oil producer has long expressed frustration with the quotas it has to follow as part of OPEC, the cartel of major state-owned oil producers.

articles.title: WHCA dinner shooter charged. And, Charles III to address Congress
articles.summary: Cole Allen, the man who tried to storm the White House Correspondents' Dinner, is being charged with trying to assassinate President Trump. And, King Charles III is set to address Congress today.

articles.title: Deadlock over Iran's nuclear program and the Strait of Hormuz cripples peace efforts
articles.summary: Two months after the U.S.-Israeli attack on Iran started the war, peace talks are on hold, with control of the Strait of Hormuz and the future of Iran's nuclear program as the two main points of conte

articles.title: 'We don't know what will happen to us': U.S. deportees in limbo in DRC
articles.summary: 15 South American migrants and asylum seekers deported from the U.S. to the DRC are now living in uncertainty in a country an with ongoing armed conflict, where they have no ties.

articles.title: Dominion still has pending lawsuits against election deniers such as Rudy Giuliani and Sidney Powell
